tree.setup: Your Chinese friend invites you to their birthday party this Saturday.
tree.setup_zh: 你的中国朋友邀请你参加这个周六的生日聚会。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个周六是我生日，你能来我家吃饭吗？
turns.text_pinyin: zhè ge zhōuliù shì wǒ shēngrì, nǐ néng lái wǒ jiā chīfàn ma?
turns.text_en: This Saturday is my birthday, can you come to my home for dinner?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Accept the invitation and ask what you can bring.

options.text_zh: 我们去看电影或者找个地方喝咖啡吧。
options.text_pinyin: wǒmen qù kàn diànyǐng huòzhě zhǎo ge dìfang hē kāfēi ba.
options.text_en: Let's go see a movie or find a place to get coffee.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to accept the invitation and ask what you can bring, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 当然可以！祝你生日快乐！我带什么来好呢？
options.text_pinyin: dāngrán kěyǐ! zhù nǐ shēngrì kuàilè! wǒ dài shénme lái hǎo ne?
options.text_en: Of course! Happy birthday! What should I bring?
options.feedback: Enthusiastic, warm, and thoughtful — offering to bring something shows consideration.

options.text_pinyin: shì de, wǒ yùdìng le. wǒ xìng lǐ.
options.text_en: Yes, I have a reservation. My surname is Li.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to accept the invitation and ask what you can bring, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢！什么都不用带，人来就好了！
turns.text_pinyin: xièxie! shénme dōu bú yòng dài, rén lái jiù hǎo le!
turns.text_en: Thanks! You don't need to bring anything, just come!

options.text_zh: 那怎么行，我带一个蛋糕过去吧。
options.text_pinyin: nà zěnme xíng, wǒ dài yī ge dàngāo guòqù ba.
options.text_en: How can I come empty-handed? I'll bring a cake.
options.feedback: 那怎么行 is a natural way to politely insist — bringing a cake to a birthday is always welcome.

options.text_zh: 好啊，我很想去！你想看几点的？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo a, wǒ hěn xiǎng qù! nǐ xiǎng kàn jǐ diǎn de?
options.text_en: Sure, I'd love to go! What time do you want to see?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to insist on bringing something anyway, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 哇，成都看起来很漂亮！你去了哪些地方？
options.text_pinyin: wā, chéngdū kàn qǐlai hěn piàoliang! nǐ qù le nǎxiē dìfang?
options.text_en: Wow, Chengdu looks beautiful! What places did you visit?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to insist on bringing something anyway, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

tree.cultural_note: When a Chinese host says 什么都不用带 (don't bring anything), it's almost always a polite gesture — you should still bring something. Common gifts include fruit, cake, milk, or tea. Arriving truly empty-handed (空手 kōngshǒu) can feel awkward. This back-and-forth of offering and declining is a core part of Chinese social etiquette called 客气 (kèqi).
